question: How to achieve routing symmetry for differential antenna paths requiring ±0.5dB gain match?
options: A
Staggered wiring is adopted and the ground plane connection path is optimized
B
Use equimetric path routing but allow different layer traversal methods
C
Use mirrored layouts and traces to ensure symmetry in the electrical environment
D
Reduce the risk of coupling interference by separating trace channels
E
None of the above options are correct

question: How to layout multi-layer flex PCBs to reduce bending impact on signal integrity?
options: A
Arrange the high-speed signal on the outer layer to facilitate monitoring and adjustment
B
Route along the main bend direction to avoid sharp turns and stress concentration
C
Use widened copper wire to reduce resistance, but allow path cross-routing
D
Keep the traces short and evenly distributed across multiple flexible sections
E
None of the above options are correct

question: How to route signal layers in multilayer magnetically shielded modules to reduce eddy currents?
options: A
Wire as close as possible to the shield layer to enhance the electromagnetic restraint effect
B
Avoid switching between layers and maintain the integrity of a single layer of continuous traces
C
Use interlayer traces and staggered interlayer orientation to avoid closed loops
D
Arrange the signal wire close to the power line and return the ground to the ground
E
None of the above options are correct

question: How to layout layers in systems with HV motor drivers and low-voltage sensors to reduce coupling?
options: A
Route the high-voltage drive and the low-voltage signal side by side, and unify the ground plane to avoid floating ground
B
Distribute the high-voltage drive signal on the inner layer and place the low-voltage acquisition on the outer layer to shorten the path
C
The high-voltage traces are arranged by the outer layer, and the inner layer analog signal is completely isolated through the layer
D
Mix analog and digital signals and reduce impedance interference with thick copper layers
E
None of the above options are correct

question: In rigid-flex multilayer PCBs, how to layout to avoid stress concentration and microcracks?
options: A
The high-voltage traces are arranged by the outer layer, and the inner layer analog signal is completely isolated through the formation
B
Distribute the high-voltage drive signal on the inner layer and place the low-voltage acquisition on the outer layer to shorten the path
C
Wire the high-voltage and low-voltage lines adjacent to each other, and shield the interference through thick layer connections
D
All signal lines are arranged in the middle layer, and the outer power supply layer is used to form a symmetrical structure
E
None of the above options are correct

question: In AI audio edge processors, how to layout microphone arrays and acoustic isolation to reduce echo interference?
options: A
Use symmetrical trace structure in the flexible zone and encrypt the ground grid to disperse stress
B
Use fan-out structure transitions in the rigid-flex zone to avoid overlapping vias with components
C
The main control components are arranged centrally in the flexible area to reduce the length of the wiring span
D
Use continuous blind holes in the connection area to improve structural strength and thermal stability
E
None of the above options are correct

question: In multimedia boards with USB and HDMI, how to layout differential pairs and coupling references?
options: A
HDMI and USB differential cables are layered to ensure continuous impedance and prevent cross-coupling
B
All differential pairs are centralized on the same layer, simplifying the reflow path and sharing the reference layer
C
USB differential pairs are laid out close to the HDMI cabling area to reduce signal interference paths
D
Place the reference stratum on the top layer to reduce coupling issues through lower traces
E
None of the above options are correct

question: In multi-layer connector bus systems, how to layout vertical channels and filters?
options: A
The upper and lower channels are wired in parallel, and the filter components are centrally placed at the power supply inlet
B
Staggered layered wiring of upper and lower channels, filter capacitors close to the convergence point and add ground through-hole walls
C
All channel pins are placed on the same layer to reduce insertion loss, and the filter is close to the main control chip
D
Use daisy-chain structure to connect the upper and lower channels to shield interference through centralized grounding
E
None of the above options are correct

question: In embedded boards with board-to-board connectors, how to layout critical signals and layer routing?
options: A
Route the high-speed signal close to the connector, and the direction between the layers is orthogonal to avoid turning coupling
B
The key signal is distributed at the bottom layer to reduce interference, and filtering capacitors are concentrated in the middle layer
C
All signal traces are in the same direction to improve the consistency of wiring, and the connectors are arranged up and down
D
Extend the high-speed path with serpentine traces to enhance tolerance matching
E
None of the above options are correct

question: In automotive gateway controllers, how to layout LIN, CAN, and Ethernet PHYs to avoid interference?
options: A
The interface of each communication protocol is partitioned, and the signal line is far away and shielded from the ground wire
B
The layout of LIN and CAN is co-located to improve compatibility, and the PHY is concentrated close to the main control chip
C
PHY components are distributed in the unified area of the bottom layer and the signal is crossed through the top trace
D
The bus interfaces are staggered and symmetrically distributed with the power supply plane
E
None of the above options are correct

question: In EEG front-end PCBs, how to layout electrodes, amplifiers, and shielding?
options: A
The amplifier is arranged in the center of the board, and the electrode input is connected to the extension trace
B
The electrode wire is routed close to the digital processing area, and the uniform shield is located on the bottom layer
C
The electrode signal is far away from the digital region, and the amplifier is close to the input and ground shielded
D
The shield is moved down to the power plane to share the reflow path with the amplifier
E
None of the above options are correct

question: In HMI boards with capacitive touch and shielding, how to layout signals and shields to prevent false touches?
options: A
The touch signal cable is routed snugly against the edge of the shield to improve response sensitivity
B
Use the top trace close to the edge of the panel with the shield suspended to prevent induced interference
C
The touch signal is routed away from the edge of the shield, and the grounded porous structure stabilizes the interlayer coupling
D
The shielding layer and the touch cable are arranged overlapping, and the coupling is blocked by a thick dielectric layer
E
None of the above options are correct

question: In controllers with isolated CAN, how to layout isolators, transceivers, and ground domains?
options: A
The isolation chip and the transceiver are arranged on the same side, and the numbers and CAN are strictly partitioned and continuous references are maintained
B
Cross-arrangement of isolated and non-isolated components, using uniform grounding to simplify wiring
C
The interface chips are concentrated near the main control, and the reference ground is uniformly connected through the grounding bridge
D
Place the CAN transceiver in the digital area with the isolation layer near the power input
E
None of the above options are correct

question: In multi-channel temperature acquisition boards, how to layout thermocouple amps, cold-junction sensors, and shielding?
options: A
The cold-end compensation sensor is centrally arranged on the edge of the board, and the amplifier is located around the main control chip
B
The amplifier is arranged close to the thermocouple end, with the compensation sensor adjacent to the amplifier and shielded from the critical traces
C
All sensitive signals are uniformly distributed on the bottom layer, and the shielding path is formed through the power supply layer
D
The shielding layer is placed above the digital area, and the hot and cold signals are cross-wired
E
None of the above options are correct

question: In hybrid IR + laser rangefinders, how to layout detectors, lasers, and shielding?
options: A
Infrared and laser sources are arranged side by side, and the signal layer is unified to enhance the synchronization accuracy
B
Infrared and laser sources are staggered, with metal shielding in the middle, and analog signals are layered and routed
C
The laser emitter is arranged close to the power supply area, and the infrared receiver is placed directly below it
D
All sensing components are distributed in the same area, spatially isolated by a thick media layer
E
None of the above options are correct

question: In automotive Ethernet boards, how to layout PHY, EMI filters, and shield connections?
options: A
The PHY is arranged close to the RJ45 interface, the filter is connected in series to the receiving path, and the shield is completely covered by the grounding copper skin
B
EMI filters are distributed on the power supply side, and PHY chips are placed near the main control
C
The shield is connected to the signal reference layer, and a transition buffer is reserved between the PHY and the RJ45
D
PHY is arranged away from the interface to optimize the thermal diffusion path, and the filter is connected in parallel to the signal line
E
None of the above options are correct

question: In laser-ranging obstacle sensors, how to layout TX control, echo sampling, and shielding?
options: A
The emission and acquisition modules are symmetrically arranged in the upper and lower layers, providing a common reference through the strata
B
The laser drive and acquisition unit are divided into partitions, and the shield is connected by multi-point grounding
C
The laser is driven by the same layer as the acquisition neighbor, and the shield layer surrounds the acquisition path and grounds at a single point
D
The echo path is adjusted by using a serpentine trace, and the shielding structure is set at the bottom edge
E
None of the above options are correct

question: In multilayer boards, how to place ESD parts for better immunity?
options: A
ESD devices are placed close to the main chip, and the traces are extended to isolate the interference source
B
The ESD device is arranged close to the interface, with short and straight traces, supplemented by grounding copper skin to construct a low-resistance channel
C
ESD protection circuit is cross-routed with signal path, and layered grounding is used to improve versatility
D
All ESD components are distributed in a unified area, close to the power supply area
E
None of the above options are correct

question: In CAN-FD/LIN diagnostic modules, how to layout transceivers, protection, and MCU IO?
options: A
The physical layer chip is arranged close to the interface, the TVS and resistive protection are connected in parallel, and the impedance at the MCU connection is matched
B
The physical layer chip is arranged on the MCU side, and the TVS protection circuit is concentrated next to the power supply module
C
All interface devices are distributed near the main control, and the protection components are connected in series with the signal line
D
The physical layer chip is arranged diagonally from the MCU, and the protective device is placed in the middle of the board to buffer interference
E
None of the above options are correct

question: In wireless relays, how to layout transceivers, controller, and isolation layer?
options: A
The relay transceiver is arranged close to the power supply, and the control module is arranged on the edge of the board to prevent interference
B
The transceiver is arranged by the antenna interfaces on both sides, the control module is centered, and the isolation layer is used for channel ground reference
C
All RF channels are centrally traced on the top layer, and the control module is located on the lower layer to improve reliability
D
The switching control and the transceiver are arranged side by side, and the antenna signal lines are staggered through the shielded area
E
None of the above options are correct

question: In RF front-ends, how to layout dual antennas, LNA, and RF switch array?
options: A
The upper and lower antennas are symmetrically wired, the LNA is close to the interface area, and the RF switch is arranged at the end of the signal
B
The antenna and LNA are arranged on different layers, and the RF switch is close to the MCU for unified control
C
The upper and lower antennas are arranged in layers, with the LNA adjacent to the antenna port and the RF switch placed in the middle switching channel
D
All devices are centrally located next to the main control to simplify trace paths and power supply
E
None of the above options are correct

question: In HV insulation monitors, how to layout input, isolation, and processor?
options: A
The sampling interface partition is arranged close to the monitoring point, and the isolation device is located between the analog and digital regions, and the processor is placed in the center
B
All sampling channels are uniformly directed out to the central area, cross-isolated through the power layer to reduce module interference
C
Configure small isolated devices next to each channel and connect them to the peripheral controller block with redundant cabling
D
The processor is arranged at the high-voltage interface and arranged in parallel with the isolation channel to realize the centralized control logic
E
None of the above options are correct

question: In marine sensor boards, how to layout waterproof connectors, sensors, and shielding?
options: A
The connector is arranged by the edge encapsulation area, the sensor is distributed in the open area, and the shield surrounds the sensitive path
B
All waterproof connectors and sensors are concentrated in the central area, and the thermal stress is balanced and uniformly shielded by equidistant arrangement
C
Connectors and electrochemical sensors are distributed on both sides of the board and are connected to the shielded housing by a double seal structure for enhanced reliability
D
The sensor is distributed in the middle area of the interface with an anti-corrosion layer, and the shielding structure uses a multi-layer wrapping strategy to cover the entire line section
E
None of the above options are correct

question: In CPO communication boards, how to layout E-O converters, micro-optics, and EMI layers?
options: A
The electro-optical conversion chip is close to the SerDes arrangement, the low-light engine is close to the adapter interface, and the EMI layer covers the transmission area
B
The micro engine is integrated with the data control logic in the top-level module and coupled to the remote transmission interface via an optical waveguide to reduce losses
C
Distribute the electro-optical converter on the edge of the board, distribute it at an angle to the connector, and arrange a large area of copper layer below to suppress crosstalk
D
All optical modules are uniformly placed in the center of signal symmetry, and the EMI path is blocked by a two-way multi-point grounding structure
E
None of the above options are correct

question: In embedded passive PCBs, how to layout embedded caps/inductors, signal coupling, and stackup?
options: A
All passive devices are distributed between the peripheral channels and staggered on different layers to form a tunable coupling structure
B
The buried/buried sensing area is placed in the middle layer, the signal line is kept vertically routed, and the stacked design ensures the coupling stability
C
Symmetrically copper-paved copper layer forms an equivalent capacitor network, and the signal line trace direction is freely adjusted according to the device layout
D
The buried capacitance and sensing devices are distributed in the area close to the surface, and the signal modulation capability is enhanced by multi-layer interleaved traces
E
None of the above options are correct

question: In HMI boards with touch and proximity, how to layout driver, sensor, and EMC structures?
options: A
The driver signal is layered and staggered, and the sensing area covers the entire display module area, and intermittent ground shielding is set at the edge
B
The induction layer is concentrated in the middle area, and the EMC structure adopts an asymmetric grounding method to cover the periphery and attach an anti-disturbance capacitor array
C
The touch drive is close to the FPC connector, the induction layer is symmetrically distributed, and the EMC structure surrounds the signal area to prevent leakage
D
The touch driver and display control are uniformly wired to the processor, and the EMC structure is connected to each layer of grounding in a through-hole array
E
None of the above options are correct
